column2: Jarvis is the human-agent collaboration and learning-loop protocol. It defines WorkSession, Policy, Request, Review, Takeover, Contribution, EvidenceManifest, LearningRecord, MemoryProposal, and SkillProposal so existing agents and host applications collaborate with humans without replacing their runtimes.

Near-term priority: make Jarvis OpenAPI-ready: protocol lock, OpenAPI 3.1 communication binding, version and capability negotiation, zero-trust security model, portable export, and conformance entry fixtures. Compatible example starts only after the protocol contract and conformance entry rules are stable. Keep scope narrow: protocol records first, OpenAPI contract second, conformance third, adapters fourth, compatible example last.

column3: Turn Jarvis into an implementation-ready protocol lock.
column4:
- freeze v0.1 protocol lock
- align README, principles, architecture, roadmap
- keep language direct and host-generic
- confirm object names and lifecycle
column5:
- define OpenAPI entry gate
- list open OpenAPI questions
- identify version, capability, extension, and error-model gaps
- prepare OpenAPI component and path layout
- define zero-trust headers and security schemes
column6:
- defer adapter implementation
- name adapter questions only
- preserve runtime independence
column7:
- keep host implementation work out of active scope
- define compatible-example prerequisites only
column8: Protocol lock is the source for OpenAPI work, OpenAPI 3.1 is the machine-readable communication contract, and no compatible example enters this week.

column3: Create the v0.1 OpenAPI communication contract.
column4:
- finalize required object meanings
- lock WorkSession state transitions
- lock Request, Review, Takeover states
- lock Contribution and Evidence terms
column5:
- create OpenAPI 3.1 document skeleton
- create components.schemas for core objects
- create path layout for core operations
- define protocol error responses
- define required security headers
column6:
- document adapter contract questions
- define AgentWorker adapter surface
- define tool/action to PolicyDecision mapping
- define limitations field for unsupported concepts
column7:
- keep host implementation work out of active scope
- define compatible-example gate conditions only
column8: A developer reads the OpenAPI contract and knows exactly how to create a valid WorkSession, Request, Review, Contribution, EvidenceManifest, and LearningRecord.

column3: Make conformance strong enough to prove compatibility.
column4:
- write compatibility rules
- define extension namespace rules
- define version negotiation
- define capability negotiation
column5:
- build golden-path fixtures
- build failing fixtures
- validate status transitions
- validate export does not require product-private fields
column6:
- create CLI/existing-agent adapter plan
- capture messages/actions as JarvisEvents
- capture tool use as PolicyDecision and Evidence
- capture human correction as Review
column7:
- keep compatible example out of active scope
- record compatible-example prerequisites from conformance only
- generate protocol-only sample WorkSession export
- document host-only assumptions separately
column8: Conformance rejects missing policy, missing review, stale takeover continuation, unattributed contribution, weak evidence, and silent learning mutation.

column3: Prove Jarvis with conformance fixtures and one adapter contract.
column4:
- refine protocol from real fixture pain
- keep object names stable
- document compatibility decisions
- update protocol lock only for real contradictions
column5:
- run OpenAPI examples against host export
- run OpenAPI examples against CLI/adapter export
- compare both exports for same core semantics
- record gaps as conformance issues
column6:
- implement proof adapter path
- HumanWorker objective -> AgentWorker action
- blocked action -> Request
- human response -> Review or Takeover
column7:
- prepare compatible examples after the conformance gate
- define product-private fields that stay out of export
- update simulation narrative if needed
column8: Two different host or adapter shapes produce valid Jarvis records for the same collaboration loop without sharing runtime, UI, storage, or execution stack.

column3: Freeze v0.1 alpha and prepare compatible-example review.
column4:
- close protocol lock gaps
- publish compatibility notes
- summarize decisions and non-goals
- prepare v0.2 evidence/learning backlog
column5:
- freeze v0.1 OpenAPI contract
- publish conformance checklist
- publish valid/invalid examples
- define migration policy draft
column6:
- write adapter guide
- document CLI/existing-agent adapter
- document host implementation guide
- identify next two adapter targets
column7:
- publish protocol proof walkthrough URL
- package compatible-example readiness notes
- prepare team walkthrough
- collect review notes into next-cycle issues
column8: Jarvis has a v0.1 alpha package of protocol docs, OpenAPI contract, examples, conformance fixtures, compatible-example readiness, and one existing-agent adapter contract.

column3: Expand only from conformance results and compatible-example evidence.
column4:
- harden object model from real adapters
- add migration rules
- add public implementation checklist
- prepare v0.2 evidence and learning beta
column5:
- richer EvidenceManifest
- stronger Contribution taxonomy
- review-state workflows
- memory and skill compatibility rules
column6:
- more agent adapters
- MCP/A2A/AG-UI integration notes
- SDK-neutral adapter examples
- compatibility badges
column7:
- additional compatible examples
- public proof refresh
- team onboarding sheet
- protocol review sessions
column8: Expansion is justified by valid exports, conformance failures, adapter pain, and compatible-example evidence, not by unproven feature expansion.
